title: It's Skills, Not Character
content: I used to be ashamed of my money situation. Couldn't even look at my bank account. Then someone told me: 'You're not bad with money. You just never learned systems.' That reframe changed everything. It's skills, not character.
extended_content: We don't teach money in schools. We don't talk about it at home. Then we feel ashamed when we don't magically know how to do it. That's like being ashamed you can't play piano when no one ever gave you lessons. Money management is a skill. Skills can be learned. You're not broken. You're just untrained. And that's fixable.
when_to_tell: when someone expresses shame about their finances
lesson: Financial skill can be learned at any age
